update.checkTitle: Check if new version is available
update.needsRestart: Code updated. Please click the Restart button in the top right corner to apply changes.
update.restartMsg: Code updated to version {version}. Please click the Restart button in the top right corner to apply changes.
update.confirmMsg: Are you sure you want to update to version {version}? Service may be briefly unavailable during the update.

routing.title: Path Routing Examples
routing.description: Access different AI model providers through different path routes, supporting flexible model switching
routing.tip1: Instant Switching: Change the URL path to switch between different AI model providers
routing.tip2: Client Configuration: Set the API endpoint to the corresponding path in clients like Cherry-Studio, NextChat, Cline
routing.tip3: Cross-protocol: Support calling Claude models via OpenAI protocol or vice versa

basic.title: Basic Settings
basic.apiKeyGeneratedAndCopied: New API Key generated and copied

provider.title: Model Providers (Multiple)
provider.required: Select providers to initialize on startup (at least one required)
provider.requiredSingle: At least one preload provider must be selected

proxy.title: Proxy Settings
proxy.urlNote: Supports HTTP, HTTPS and SOCKS5 proxies. Leave empty to not use proxy
proxy.enabledProvidersNote: Select providers that need to be accessed through proxy. Unselected providers will connect directly
proxy.tlsSidecarNote: When enabled, selected providers will be forwarded through Go uTLS sidecar, perfectly simulating Chrome TLS/H2 fingerprint to bypass Cloudflare (requires restart)

advanced.title: Advanced Configuration
advanced.credentialSwitchMaxRetriesNote: Max retries after credential auth error (401/403), default 5
advanced.warmupTargetNote: Number of nodes to auto-refresh on startup, default 0
advanced.refreshConcurrencyPerProviderNote: Max parallel refresh tasks per provider, default 1
advanced.loginExpiryNote: Admin console login token validity, default 3600 seconds (1 hour)
advanced.poolFilePath: Provider Pool Config File Path (cannot be empty)
advanced.poolNote: Use empty node if using default client authorization config
advanced.maxErrorCountNote: Provider will be marked unhealthy after consecutive errors reach this count, default 10
advanced.poolSizeLimitNote: Max healthy credentials per provider type for rotation. 0 means unlimited, use all healthy credentials
advanced.fallbackChainNote: When all accounts of a provider type are unhealthy, automatically switch to configured fallback type. JSON format, key is primary type, value is fallback type array (priority ordered)
advanced.fallbackChainInvalid: Fallback chain configuration format invalid, please enter valid JSON
advanced.modelFallbackMappingNote: Map specific models to other protocol providers when primary provider is unavailable. Lower priority than Fallback Chain above. JSON format.
advanced.modelFallbackMappingInvalid: Model fallback mapping configuration format invalid, please enter valid JSON
advanced.systemPromptReplacementsNote: Sequentially apply content replacements to system prompts (both request and file).
advanced.adminPasswordPlaceholder: Set admin password (leave empty to not change)
advanced.adminPasswordNote: Used to protect admin console access, requires re-login after change

healthCheck.title: Scheduled Health Check
healthCheck.intervalNote: In milliseconds, min 60000ms (1 min), max 3600000ms (1 hour), can input manually or use shortcut buttons
healthCheck.providerTypesNote: Select provider types for scheduled health check, leave empty for no checks

log.title: Log Settings
log.maxFilesNote: Number of recent log files to keep

providers.title: Provider Pool Management
providers.poolDescription: Use empty node for default path configuration
providers.searchPlaceholder: Search provider name or node content...

common.fileTypeError: Unsupported file type. Please select JSON, TXT, KEY, PEM, P12, or PFX.

plugins.title: Plugin Management
plugins.description: The plugin system allows you to extend system functionality. Enabling or disabling plugins requires a service restart to take effect.
plugins.restartRequired: Changes saved, requires restart to take effect

models.title: Available Models
models.note: Click model name to copy to clipboard

customModels.title: Custom Model Management
customModels.description: Custom Model Management supports three use cases: 1. Define default parameters for an existing model; 2. Map one model to another provider or actual model; 3. Create a new model and add it to the model list. New models require a service restart to take effect.
customModels.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete model {id}?

intro.title: Introduction
intro.desc: AIClient2API is an API proxy service that breaks client restrictions, converting free large models like Gemini, Antigravity, Qwen Code, and Kiro into standard OpenAI-compatible interfaces that any application can call.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: Q: What to do if request returns 404 error?
faq.a1: A: Check if the API path is correct. Some clients automatically append paths to Base URL, causing duplication. Check the actual request URL in the console and remove redundant path parts.
faq.q2: Q: What to do if request returns 429 error?
faq.a2: A: 429 means request rate is too high. Configure multiple accounts in the provider pool with polling; or configure Fallback chain for cross-type degradation.
faq.q3: Q: What to do if OAuth authorization fails?
faq.a3: A: Ensure OAuth callback ports are accessible (Gemini: 8085, Antigravity: 8086, Kiro: 19876-19880). Docker users need to map these ports correctly.
faq.q4: Q: How to view available models?
faq.a4: A: Click Available Models in the sidebar to view all models supported by configured providers. Click model name to copy.
faq.q5: Q: What to do if streaming response is interrupted?
faq.a5: A: Check network stability, increase client request timeout. If using proxy, ensure it supports long connections.
faq.q6: Q: What to do if request returns No available and healthy providers error?
faq.a6: A: This means all providers of the corresponding type are unavailable. Check provider health status in Provider Pools page, confirm OAuth credentials are not expired, or configure Fallback chain for automatic switching to backup providers.
faq.q7: Q: What to do if request returns 403 Forbidden error?
faq.a7: A: 403 means access denied. First check node status in Provider Pools page. If node health check is normal, you can ignore this error. Other possible causes include: insufficient account permissions, limited API Key permissions, regional access restrictions, expired credentials, etc.
faq.q8: Q: Why should I enable OAuth Token Auto-Refresh?
faq.a8: A: OAuth tokens usually have an expiration (e.g., 1 hour). When enabled, the system automatically refreshes tokens in the background, ensuring 24/7 stable service and avoiding request failures due to expired tokens.
faq.q9: Q: What is the impact of not enabling Preload Model Providers on token maintenance?
faq.a9: A: The system only performs auto-refresh for pre-initialized providers. If a provider is not pre-initialized, its token will not be automatically refreshed in the background, which may lead to expiration if not used for a long time. It is recommended to add frequently used providers to the pre-initialized list.
